reasoning: By looking at the most recent progress on Nipah virus vaccines, we can see that the leading candidate (ChAdOx1 NipahB) only began Phase 1 clinical trials in January 2024. This trial is expected to last 18 months, concluding around mid-2025. As of early 2025, there are no Nipah virus vaccines in Phase 2 or Phase 3 trials. Vaccine development typically requires rigorous and time-consuming Phase 2 and Phase 3 efficacy trials following Phase 1 safety trials. Even under accelerated emergency pathways (such as during the Covid-19 pandemic), developing, approving, and formally recognizing a vaccine within a few months of completing a Phase 1 trial is exceptionally unlikely. Since no vaccine is currently close to licensure, the likelihood that Wikipedia will update its 'List of infectious diseases' to show a 'Yes' for a Nipah vaccine by the end of 2025 is effectively zero. Using the lowest allowed probability boundary of 0.05, slightly increasing for the more distant dates as guided by standard forecasting uncertainty rules.
